sources.notes: Segments are paired to create extra marked segments, and the objective is maximum total segment length.
Official reference code sorts by l+r, selects half of the segments by this key, and handles the odd leftover by scanning split candidates.

annotation.summary: Pair unmarked segments to create extra marked segments and maximize the final total length of original and created segments.
annotation.constraints: Sum of n over tests is at most 2e5, requiring sorting plus linear postprocessing.
annotation.core_idea: The original segment lengths are fixed. For each created segment, one chosen interval supplies a left endpoint and the other supplies a right endpoint, so for an even number of intervals the pairing value is maximized by choosing exactly n/2 intervals with minimum l+r to act as left-side suppliers and the rest as right-side suppliers. For odd n, one interval is left unpaired; sorting by l+r lets us scan the possible leftover around the split and keep the best correction.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) per test case.

solution_variants.name: sort-by-endpoint-sum
solution_variants.summary: Add the fixed original lengths, sort intervals by l+r, choose the lower half as left suppliers, and scan the odd case's unpaired interval.

tags.evidence: The decisive ordering is by l+r; after sorting, the optimal half split and odd leftover corrections are computed directly.
The optimal construction greedily assigns intervals with smallest l+r to the side that pays l instead of gaining r.
The formula isolates the fixed original length contribution and only optimizes the extra contribution from created segments.
